summary: Foundation enemies now use three Quaternius CC0 monster GLBs as static visuals with existing health, ray-hit damage, destruction, and debug reporting preserved.
observations: The foundation level has three cube enemy placeholders.
Each cube owns a shared Health instance and reports through the debug enemy snapshot.
The spawn-lane cube is destroyable by normal hold-fire input in local browser smoke.
No level-specific external enemy brief has been created.
Visible cube enemies were replaced by Mushnub, Pink Slime, and Goleling GLB visuals from Quaternius via Poly Pizza.
The runtime keeps invisible box hit proxies for weapon ray damage instead of raycasting render meshes.
Enemy asset IDs, byte sizes, hashes, source URLs, and CC0 license records are documented in the source ledger.
decisions: Enemy assets must be judged by silhouette, animation coverage, hit proxies, audio hooks, and mobile performance.
Use recurring enemy types for recognition and unique variants for level identity.
Keep the cube enemy system as the first behavior contract until external enemy assets replace the placeholder meshes.
Keep GLB visuals separate from hit proxies for this no-movement enemy slice.
Do not add movement or animation playback until enemy behavior and mobile readability are reviewed.
caughtIssues: Current cube enemies validate damage and destruction only; they do not block player movement or animate hit/death states.
Enemy GLBs are static visual replacements only; movement, hit reactions, and death animations remain future work.
risks: Enemy assets without readable attack and hit states will make mobile combat feel unfair.
Replacing cubes with external assets can break ray-hit readability if hit proxies are not authored deliberately.
Static enemy models may read as props without later animation, hit flash, or death feedback.
